race.name: Pepsi Challenge
race.tagline: 50 kilometers on a World Cup venue in Minnesota's Iron Range — where the sport's fastest surfaces meet the Midwest's deepest competitive field.

vitals.registration: Opens in fall; does not typically sell out
vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations with warm drinks, energy food, and wax support

climate.description: Late February on the Iron Range delivers consistent cold and reliable snow. Temperatures range from -15°C to -2°C. The area's inland location and northern latitude provide excellent snow preservation. Wind exposure varies by course section.
challenges: Iron Range cold snaps can push temps well below -20°C
Exposed ridgeline sections with wind chill
Variable wax conditions through the day as temps shift
Altitude is modest but hilly terrain is relentless

course.primary: Multi-loop course at Giants Ridge Recreation Area, a former World Cup venue. The terrain is demanding with constant rolling hills, punchy climbs, and fast technical descents through mixed forest. World Cup-standard grooming on purpose-built trails.
course.surface: Machine-groomed World Cup-standard trails with classic tracks and skating lanes
course.grooming: World Cup venue grooming standards — among the best trail surfaces in North America
features: Giants Ridge hosted FIS World Cup events
Relentless rolling terrain with no flat recovery sections
Both classic and freestyle distances offered
Technical descents require strong downhill skills
Iron Range mining country setting with boreal forest

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The World Cup venue pedigree elevates this event above typical regional races. Elite-level grooming and a technically demanding course attract a serious competitive field. Remote location limits accessibility.

history.summary: The Pepsi Challenge has been a fixture of Minnesota Nordic racing since 1987, leveraging the world-class trail infrastructure at Giants Ridge. The venue's FIS World Cup hosting credentials give the race a pedigree unmatched by most citizen races in North America. Set in the Iron Range — the historic mining region of northern Minnesota — the race draws a deep competitive field from the Midwest's vibrant Nordic community.
notable_facts: Giants Ridge hosted FIS Cross-Country World Cup events
Set in Minnesota's Iron Range, the historic mining heartland
One of the few American citizen races on a World Cup venue
Offers both classic and freestyle 50km options
Part of the American Ski Marathon Series
